quotes.quote_text: “ I do not mind his not talking to Mrs. Long , ”

context.quote_text: “ that he never speaks much unless among his intimate acquaintance . With _ them _ he is remarkably agreeable . ”

“ I do not believe a word of it , my dear . If he had been so very agreeable , he would have talked to Mrs. Long . But I can guess how it was ; everybody says that he is eat up with pride , and I dare say he had heard somehow that Mrs. Long does not keep a carriage , and had to come to the ball in a hack chaise . ”
“ but I wish he had danced with Eliza . ”
“ Another time , Lizzy , ”

quotes.quote_text: “ but I wish he had danced with Eliza . ”

context.quote_text: “ I do not believe a word of it , my dear . If he had been so very agreeable , he would have talked to Mrs. Long . But I can guess how it was ; everybody says that he is eat up with pride , and I dare say he had heard somehow that Mrs. Long does not keep a carriage , and had to come to the ball in a hack chaise . ”
“ I do not mind his not talking to Mrs. Long , ”
“ Another time , Lizzy , ”
“ I would not dance with _ him _ , if I were you . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ His pride , ”

context.quote_text: “ I would not dance with _ him _ , if I were you . ”
“ I believe , ma’am , I may safely promise you _ never _ to dance with him . ”

“ does not offend _ me _ so much as pride often does , because there is an excuse for it . One can not wonder that so very fine a young man , with family , fortune , everything in his favour , should think highly of himself . If I may so express it , he has a _ right _ to be proud . ”
“ That is very true , ”

quotes.quote_text: “ does not offend _ me _ so much as pride often does , because there is an excuse for it . One can not wonder that so very fine a young man , with family , fortune , everything in his favour , should think highly of himself . If I may so express it , he has a _ right _ to be proud . ”

context.quote_text: “ I believe , ma’am , I may safely promise you _ never _ to dance with him . ”
“ His pride , ”
“ That is very true , ”
“ and I could easily forgive _ his _ pride , if he had not mortified _ mine _ . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ If I were as rich as Mr. Darcy , ”

context.quote_text: “ Pride , ”

“ is a very common failing , I believe . By all that I have ever read , I am convinced that it is very common indeed ; that human nature is particularly prone to it , and that there are very few of us who do not cherish a feeling of self - complacency on the score of some quality or other , real or imaginary . Vanity and pride are different things , though the words are often used synonymously . A person may be proud without being vain . Pride relates more to our opinion of ourselves ; vanity to what we would have others think of us . ”
“ I should not care how proud I was . I would keep a pack of foxhounds , and drink a bottle of wine every day . ”
“ Then you would drink a great deal more than you ought , ”

quotes.quote_text: “ I should not care how proud I was . I would keep a pack of foxhounds , and drink a bottle of wine every day . ”

context.quote_text: “ is a very common failing , I believe . By all that I have ever read , I am convinced that it is very common indeed ; that human nature is particularly prone to it , and that there are very few of us who do not cherish a feeling of self - complacency on the score of some quality or other , real or imaginary . Vanity and pride are different things , though the words are often used synonymously . A person may be proud without being vain . Pride relates more to our opinion of ourselves ; vanity to what we would have others think of us . ”
“ If I were as rich as Mr. Darcy , ”
“ Then you would drink a great deal more than you ought , ”
“ and if I were to see you at it , I should take away your bottle directly . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ Did not you think , Mr. Darcy , that I expressed myself uncommonly well just now , when I was teasing Colonel Forster to give us a ball at Meryton ? ”

context.quote_text: “ But if he does it any more , I shall certainly let him know that I see what he is about . He has a very satirical eye , and if I do not begin by being impertinent myself , I shall soon grow afraid of him . ”
“ The entreaties of several ”
“ With great energy ; but it is a subject which always makes a lady energetic . ”
“ You are severe on us . ”

“ You are severe on us . ”
“ It will be _ her _ turn soon to be teased , ”

quotes.quote_text: “ It will be _ her _ turn soon to be teased , ”

context.quote_text: “ With great energy ; but it is a subject which always makes a lady energetic . ”
“ You are severe on us . ”
“ I am going to open the instrument , Eliza , and you know what follows . ”

“ You are a very strange creature by way of a friend!--always wanting me to play and sing before anybody and everybody ! If my vanity had taken a musical turn , you would have been invaluable ; but as it is , I would really rather not sit down before those who must be in the habit of hearing the very best performers . ”

If my vanity had taken a musical turn , you would have been invaluable ; but as it is , I would really rather not sit down before those who must be in the habit of hearing the very best performers . ”
“ Very well ; if it must be so , it must . ”

If my vanity had taken a musical turn , you would have been invaluable ; but as it is , I would really rather not sit down before those who must be in the habit of hearing the very best performers . ”

“ There is a very fine old saying , which everybody here is of course familiar with--‘Keep your breath to cool your porridge,’--and I shall keep mine to swell my song . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ Very well ; if it must be so , it must . ”

context.quote_text: “ I am going to open the instrument , Eliza , and you know what follows . ”

“ You are a very strange creature by way of a friend!--always wanting me to play and sing before anybody and everybody ! If my vanity had taken a musical turn , you would have been invaluable ; but as it is , I would really rather not sit down before those who must be in the habit of hearing the very best performers . ”
“ There is a very fine old saying , which everybody here is of course familiar with--‘Keep your breath to cool your porridge,’--and I shall keep mine to swell my song . ”
“ What a charming amusement for young people this is , Mr. Darcy ! There is nothing like dancing , after all . I consider it as one of the first refinements of polished societies . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ Pray do , my dear Miss Lucas , ”

context.quote_text: “ I am glad you are come , for there is such fun here ! What do you think has happened this morning ? Mr. Collins has made an offer to Lizzy , and she will not have him . ”
“ they entered the breakfast room ”
“ for nobody is on my side , nobody takes part with me ; I am cruelly used , nobody feels for my poor nerves . ”
“ Ay , there she comes , ”

quotes.quote_text: “ for nobody is on my side , nobody takes part with me ; I am cruelly used , nobody feels for my poor nerves . ”

context.quote_text: “ they entered the breakfast room ”
“ Pray do , my dear Miss Lucas , ”
“ Ay , there she comes , ”

“ looking as unconcerned as may be , and caring no more for us than if we were at York , provided she can have her own way . But I tell you what , Miss Lizzy , if you take it into your head to go on refusing every offer of marriage in this way , you will never get a husband at all -- and I am sure I do not know who is to maintain you when your father is dead . _ I _ shall not be able to keep you -- and so I warn you . I have done with you from this very day . I told you in the library , you know , that I should never speak to you again , and you will find me as good as my word . I have no pleasure in talking to undutiful children . Not that I have much pleasure , indeed , in talking to anybody . People who suffer as I do from nervous complaints can have no great inclination for talking . Nobody can tell what I suffer ! But it is always so . Those who do not complain are never pitied . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ Why should you be surprised , my dear Eliza ? Do you think it incredible that Mr. Collins should be able to procure any woman ’s good opinion , because he was not so happy as to succeed with you ? ”

context.quote_text: “ Believe me , my dear sir , my gratitude is warmly excited by such affectionate attention ; and , depend upon it , you will speedily receive from me a letter of thanks for this as well as for every other mark of your regard during my stay in Hertfordshire . As for my fair cousins , though my absence may not be long enough to render it necessary , I shall now take the liberty of wishing them health and happiness , not excepting my cousin Elizabeth . ”
“ Engaged to Mr. Collins ! my dear Charlotte , impossible ! ”
“ I see what you are feeling , ”

“ you must be surprised , very much surprised , so lately as Mr. Collins was wishing to marry you . But when you have had time to think it all over , I hope you will be satisfied with what I have done . I am not romantic , you know . I never was . I ask only a comfortable home ; and , considering Mr. Collins ’s character , connections , and situation in life , I am convinced that my chance of happiness with him is as fair as most people can boast on entering the marriage state . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ Indeed , Mr. Bennet , ”

context.quote_text: “ Good Lord ! Sir William , how can you tell such a story ? Do not you know that Mr. Collins wants to marry Lizzy ? ”
“ _ Whenever she spoke in a low voice _ ”
“ it is very hard to think that Charlotte Lucas should ever be mistress of this house , that _ I _ should be forced to make way for _ her _ , and live to see her take my place in it ! ”
“ My dear , do not give way to such gloomy thoughts . Let us hope for better things . Let us flatter ourselves that _ I _ may be the survivor . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ it is very hard to think that Charlotte Lucas should ever be mistress of this house , that _ I _ should be forced to make way for _ her _ , and live to see her take my place in it ! ”

“ My dear , do not give way to such gloomy thoughts . Let us hope for better things . Let us flatter ourselves that _ I _ may be the survivor . ”
“ I can not bear to think that they should have all this estate . If it was not for the entail , I should not mind it . ”

quotes.quote_text: “ I should not mind anything at all . ”

context.quote_text: “ I can not bear to think that they should have all this estate . If it was not for the entail , I should not mind it . ”
“ What should not you mind ? ”
“ Let us be thankful that you are preserved from a state of such insensibility . ”

“ I never can be thankful , Mr. Bennet , for anything about the entail . How anyone could have the conscience to entail away an estate from one ’s own daughters I can not understand ; and all for the sake of Mr. Collins , too ! Why should _ he _ have it more than anybody else ? ”

quotes.quote_text: “ Let us be thankful that you are preserved from a state of such insensibility . ”

context.quote_text: “ What should not you mind ? ”
“ I should not mind anything at all . ”

“ I never can be thankful , Mr. Bennet , for anything about the entail . How anyone could have the conscience to entail away an estate from one ’s own daughters I can not understand ; and all for the sake of Mr. Collins , too ! Why should _ he _ have it more than anybody else ? ”
“ I leave it to yourself to determine , ”

quotes.quote_text: “ I never can be thankful , Mr. Bennet , for anything about the entail . How anyone could have the conscience to entail away an estate from one ’s own daughters I can not understand ; and all for the sake of Mr. Collins , too ! Why should _ he _ have it more than anybody else ? ”

context.quote_text: “ I should not mind anything at all . ”
“ Let us be thankful that you are preserved from a state of such insensibility . ”
“ I leave it to yourself to determine , ”

“ O that my dear mother had more command over herself ! she can have no idea of the pain she gives me by her continual reflections on him . But I will not repine . It can not last long . He will be forgot , and we shall all be as we were before . ”
